query: What is the current state of polling and political dynamics for Zambia's August 2026 presidential election, specifically Hakainde Hichilema's expected vote share? Include recent 2025-2026 polls, opposition coalition strength, whether Edgar Lungu/PF is running (note Lungu died in 2025), and turnout factors.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on Zambia's August 2026 presidential election:

**Hichilema's expected vote share:** The most credible poll (Namuunda Mutombo/University of Zambia-supervised, Oct 2025, n=1,009, ±3.1%) put him at **60%** (58–63% head-to-head) vs. ~35% for the combined opposition — at or slightly above his 2021 winning share of 59%. Zambia requires a 50%+1 first-round majority.

**Edgar Lungu/PF:** Lungu was deemed constitutionally ineligible and then **died on 5 June 2025**, removing the opposition's main unifying figure (followed by a prolonged burial controversy). The PF is deeply fractured into competing alliances.

**Opposition coalitions:** Highly fragmented — 14 approved candidates from ~26 filed. Main groupings: Tonse–Pamodzi Alliance (Brian Mundubile), People's Pact (Fred M'membe), Orange Alliance (Harry Kalaba); a Miles Sampa PF faction defected to UPND. Analysts (ISS) say this fragmentation makes it hard for any challenger to reach 50%+1.

**Turnout:** One July 2026 scenario projects ~82% (up from 70.61% in 2021), driven by high political interest and cost-of-living stakes, but with risks from intimidation and assembly restrictions.

**Integrity concerns:** Delimitation adding seats in UPND strongholds, rushed legislation, shortened voter-register inspection (90→14 days), and Cyber Crimes Act arrests of opposition figures — raising the risk the outcome is seen as shaped by legal manoeuvring.

The full structured findings (key facts, sources, and 11 data points) have been submitted via StructuredOutput.

sources.title: Opinion poll shows President Hakainde Hichilema headed for victory in 2026
sources.snippet: National poll (Namuunda Mutombo/UNZA, Oct 2025, n=1,009): Hichilema 60% (58-63%), opposition 35%.

sources.title: Elections 2026: is Zambia's democratic success story beginning to fray?
sources.snippet: High opposition fragmentation (14 approved candidates) weakens any opponent's chance of reaching 50%+1; delimitation and legal reforms favor UPND.

sources.title: Lungu's death and the perils of a weak opposition in Zambia
sources.snippet: Edgar Lungu died 5 June 2025; without him to unite a fragmented opposition ahead of 2026, multiparty democracy weakened.

sources.title: Alliance politics take centre stage as Zambia heads to August polls
sources.snippet: 14 candidates, 8.8M registered voters; Tonse-Pamodzi (Mundubile), People's Pact (M'membe), Orange Alliance (Kalaba); PF deeply divided after Lungu's death.

sources.title: Zambia: The Challenges of Maintaining Democratic Progress
sources.snippet: Hichilema seeking a second term; won 59% in 2021 galvanized by strong youth turnout.

sources.title: Zambia scenario Analysis predict over 82% Voter Turnout on 13th August
sources.snippet: Projected ~82% turnout (~7.2M voters) vs 70.61% (4.98M) in 2021; drivers and constraints listed.

sources.title: 2026 Zambian general election
sources.snippet: General elections due 13 August 2026 for president, National Assembly, councillors and council chairs.

sources.title: HH Wins 2026 Presidential Opinion Poll Among Top Six Popular Candidates
sources.snippet: Non-representative Zambian Post Facebook poll (67,600 participants) projected Hichilema at 55%.

key_facts: Election date: 13 August 2026 (president, National Assembly, councillors, council chairs), with ~8.79 million registered voters (53.1% women) and 13,529 polling stations.
The most credible poll (Namuunda Mutombo/UNZA-supervised, 2-15 Oct 2025, n=1,009, ±3.1%) put Hichilema at 60% (58-63% head-to-head) vs ~35% for the combined opposition.
Hichilema won the 2021 election with 59%; current polling is at or slightly above that level.
Zambia requires a 50%+1 majority to win in the first round; opposition fragmentation makes this hard for any single challenger to reach.
Edgar Lungu was deemed constitutionally ineligible to run and then died on 5 June 2025, removing the opposition's main unifying figure; a burial controversy followed.
14 presidential candidates were cleared to run (out of a record ~26 who filed), reflecting severe opposition fragmentation.

The PF/opposition is split into competing alliances: Tonse-Pamodzi (Brian Mundubile), People's Pact (Fred M'membe), Orange Alliance (Harry Kalaba); a Miles Sampa PF faction defected to the UPND Alliance.
One July 2026 scenario analysis projects ~82% turnout (~7.2M voters), up from 70.61% (4.98M) in 2021.

Integrity concerns favoring UPND: delimitation adding seats in strongholds (Southern Province 20->29), rushed legislation, voter-register inspection shortened from 90 to 14 days, and Cyber Crimes Act arrests of opposition figures (e.g., M'membe, March 2026).
A non-representative Zambian Post Facebook poll (67,600 self-selected participants) projected Hichilema at 55% among six leading contenders.

data_points.metric: Hichilema projected vote share (Mutombo/UNZA national poll)
Hichilema share (Zambian Post Facebook poll, non-representative)
